item.title: 9:18 am, March 21, 2026
item.description: Working out where I’ll want the most saturated reds to go.

media:content.media:description: Little tip-taps of light greys added to the metallic jaw, some bone colour to the teefs, and initial layers of dark desaturated red in the shadows of gun-hairdo and loincloth up to full Mephiston Red as the extreme highlight.

item.title: 6:38 am, March 21, 2026
item.description: Deep, seasonal sleep drifting between the jumbled wrecks of nightmares at the bottom.

item.title: 9:00 pm, March 20, 2026
item.description: Chasing ‘green’.

media:content.media:description: A much more ‘olive drab’ attempt at green via non-greens this time around, compared to the much more saturated look I tend to prefer. If I can get a vibrant enough red to contrast it maybe I’ll finally Blanchitsu something?

item.title: 1:00 pm, March 20, 2026
item.description:
Making a brief (?) step away from dwarf pal to give @Berit ‘s Zorn challenge a try. A greenskin with metal bits makes perfect sense for a palette without explicit green or metallics, right?

Am I broken?

media:content.media:description: Tool assembly: plastic putty, plastic cement, lil plastic guy. Also: paints 👉

item.title: 1:33 pm, March 18, 2026
item.description:
Just noticed that the knuckles on both of my hands are grazed and I’ve no memory of banging them against anything.

That’s totally normal, right?

item.title: 4:22 pm, March 15, 2026
item.description: Obligatory wig-mount shot.

media:content.media:description: One painting handle for the dwarf, one for the axe and top-knot, that’s always been my motto. They said I was _mad_, but who’s laughing now? The pile of background paints is accreting, fast becoming an entity with its opinions on the matter.

item.title: 1:58 pm, March 15, 2026
item.description: At some point I really need to work out what I’m doing for hands. Those in the set both have weapons, but I’d rather he was just clenching his fists while screaming into the unseen, like the rest of us. I’ll have to see what options I have that are 3D-printable.

item.title: 1:04 pm, March 15, 2026
item.description: Removing the last of the bare primer layer as I try to avoid thinking about axe and tattoo progression.

media:content.media:description: Barely visible on the mini for the time it took, we have some paint on his frilly linen undergarments and yellow crotch scarves (there are presumably more manly names for these things?) as well as stark lighting applied to boot and wraps, with some multicoloured highlights on the chest and belt armour panels. The background is a jumbled horror of paint pots, tubes, sanding sticks and masking tape.

item.title: 9:13 pm, March 14, 2026
item.description:
Apparently Spike from Gremlins wasn’t called Spike.

This has been a confusing way to end the day.

item.title: 10:27 am, March 12, 2026
item.description: … with a surprising level of birdsong still audible from my hedges.

item.title: 10:20 am, March 12, 2026
item.description: ‘Is that a truck idling outside?’ levels of rainfall.

item.title: 7:41 pm, March 4, 2026
item.description: A strange-feeling day. Like it wants to change around you somehow but you’ve no fear of it doing so.

item.title: 4:26 pm, March 3, 2026
item.description: I’ve starting sneezing. This is it, my body is breaking down, no longer capable of life under Earth’s menacing local star.

item.title: 3:57 pm, March 3, 2026
item.description:
This is the first sunny day of the year so far and my body is rejecting the light as inappropriate.

Seasonal jetlag.

item.title: 11:49 am, February 28, 2026
item.description: A little bit more axe wrangling, trying to get the big blocks in place on the upper and lower blades. Definitely needs a good old scuffing and weathering pass.

media:content.media:description: The background has been cropped out of this frontal shot of the mini so that I can see the values a bit more clearly (without the insane mess of wet palette and discarded paints which normally add a good degree of visual noise). A lower set of highlights have been added to the axe blades, green used to imply the sky/ground reflected light distinction.

item.title: 6:45 pm, February 27, 2026
item.description: Immediately sidetracked by non-metallic metal. The eventual headstone of every mini-painter.

media:content.media:description: Look. We can all agree that the tattoo is a huge and life-altering mistake that I’ll be paying for forever. So why not compound it with a massive ‘steel’ axe that will take just as long? In this shot the upper blade has been sketched in, it’s a bit too blue and not quite bright enough at the brightest point. It also definitely reads a bit ‘fantasy comic illustration’ rather than ‘real’. Which isn’t bad necessarily, but doesn’t quite fit the rest of the mini.

item.title: 5:53 pm, February 26, 2026
item.description: Finally starting into a slow expansion of the tattoo. This is going to take a billion years.

media:content.media:description: Tiny curly bits are being drawn around the central tattoo circle on the biggest left shoulder bump, white paint on dark blue. Approximately one one tenth of one percent compete. You can hear the tears falling gently, splish slash, just offscreen.

item.title: 8:28 pm, February 22, 2026
item.description: Starting to sketch out what a full-sleeve tattoo might look like for a fantasy dorf. Ever get the feeling you’ve bitten off absurdly more than you can chew?

media:content.media:description: Well. This is certainly going to be something. Probably not a success, as such. But _what_ a failure. Just _extravagant_ potential failure. A dark-blue and purple sleeve has been basecoated (if you’ll pardon the noun-verbing) on the left arm and verrry tentative little curve sketches have been started towards a triquetra on the deltoid.

item.title: 6:23 pm, February 22, 2026
item.description:
Why yes, I did just add a massively passive-agressive self-shaming /now page to my blog, thank you for asking.

https://fernseed.org/now/

media:content.media:description: A screenshot of a website ‘Right Now’ section showing ‘completed projects: 1 since April 29, 2012 (about one every 5,047 days)’. Genuinely expected a NaN result, so this is totally fine.

item.title: 4:46 pm, February 21, 2026
item.description: Beefy boi.

media:content.media:description: One (absurdly muscular) arm of the mini now has a range of tones from purple in deepest shadow through reddish browns to a small specular highlight of pure ivory on the side delt. Veins, as they say, are poppin’.
